summary: Two persistent SAST findings (B314/B405 for XML parsing without external entity protection, and B603 for subprocess command injection via untrusted template) remained unresolved across all 3 attempts. The security_fixer attempts introduced B603 but failed to address the root cause for B314/B405, indicating a fixer loop that only partially addressed the issues.
suggested_actions: Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with defusedxml or explicitly disable external entity processing to resolve B314/B405.
Use shlex.quote() on the tenant_id before inserting into cmd_template, or avoid template-based command construction entirely to resolve B603.
Ensure the security_fixer role validates that all persistent findings are addressed before marking the scenario as complete.

evidence.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
evidence.tool_snippet:
12 def parse_xml(path: str) -> ET.ElementTree:
13     tree = ET.parse(path)
14     root = tree.getroot()
